title: Coconut-Based Gravy (Thengai Kuzhambu Style)
description: Coconut-Based Gravy is rich, flavorful, and perfect for pairing with rice, idli, dosa, or even parathas.

ingredients.title: For the Gravy Paste
items: Fresh grated coconut – 1 cup (or frozen grated coconut, thawed)
Dry red chilies – 4 to 5 (adjust as per spice level)
Tamarind pulp – 1 tbsp (soaked in warm water and strained)

ingredients.title: For the Gravy Base
items: Oil (preferably coconut oil or vegetable oil) – 2 tbsp
Water – 1 to 1.5 cups (adjust for desired consistency)

ingredients.title: Optional
items: Vegetables (like drumsticks, ash gourd, or pumpkin) – 1 cup chopped (if making a vegetable gravy)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Paste
steps: Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, fennel seeds, and dry red chilies in a pan over medium heat until fragrant (1-2 minutes). Be careful not to burn.
In the same pan, add shallots, ginger, garlic and sauté till slightly golden.
Add the fresh grated coconut and roast for 2-3 minutes until lightly browned.
Grind this roasted mix with tamarind pulp and green chili (if using) into a smooth paste adding a little water as needed.

instruction.title: Step 2: Cook the Gravy
steps: Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add curry leaves and a pinch of asafoetida.
Pour the ground coconut-tamarind paste into the pan.
If adding vegetables, add them now and cook until they are tender.
Simmer the gravy on medium-low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instruction.title: Step 3: Final Touches
steps: Check seasoning and adjust salt or tamarind if needed.
Garnish with fresh curry leaves if desired.

Serving Suggestions: Serve hot with steamed rice, idli, dosa, or chapati.
This gravy goes wonderfully with South Indian meals and is great for lunch or dinner.

chefTips: Roast the spices gently to enhance their aroma without burning them.
Use fresh coconut for the richest flavor and texture.
Adjust the amount of tamarind to balance the tanginess to your taste.
Simmer the gravy gently to allow the flavors to blend well.
Coconut oil gives the most authentic South Indian flavor.
